title: Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma chuan DP&AR Department a enkawl tawh dawn
content:
1073/2026-2027

Aizawl, the 18th June 2026 :Vawiin khan sorkar notification tihchhuah niin Department of Personnel & Administrative Reforms (DP&AR) chu Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma’n a enkawl tawh dawn.

Political & Cabinet Department Notification 08.12.2023 leh 24.03.2025 a tihchhuah te chu siamremin vawiin khan notification thar tihchhuah a ni a. Mizoram Governor chuan Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma chu DP&AR enkawl turin a ruat a ni.

allocation of dpar to cm.pdf

title: Rajya Sabha MP thar atan Pu Khiangte Laltluangkima thlan tlin a ni
content:
1072/2026-2027

Aizawl, the 18th June 2026 :Pu Khiangte Laltluangkima, ZPM candidate chu vawiin khan Mizoram aiawh tur Rajya Sabha MP thar atan thlan tlin a ni. Election Commission of India (ECI) remruatna angin vawiin khan Mizoram Legislative Assembly-a MLA 40 zinga MLA 36 te chuan Mizoram aiawh tur Rajya Sabha MP thlanna atana hmun ruahman Conference Hall,  Mizoram Legislative Assembly Secretariat (Annexe building)-ah vote an thlak a ni.

Vote tla te hi tlai dar 5:00 khan ECI phalna hmuh hnuin chhiar nghal a ni a, result hi Fax-in ECI thawn niin ECI lamin result puan phalna an pek hnuah Pu Zothansanga Ralte, Mizoram Legislative Assembly-a Commissioner & Secretary leh he inthlanna atan a Returning Officer ni bawk chuan Pu Khiangte Laltluangkima chu Rajya Sabha MP atan thlan tlin a nih thu a puang a ni.

title: LAWNGTLAI BAWRHSAP-HOVIN GREEN MJA DAY PUALIN MJA LAWNGTLAI TEN THINGTIAK PHUN
content:
1071/2026-2027

Lawngtlai,18th June, 2026:Mizoram Journalists' Association Lawngtlai District ten kum 2016 atanga kumtin an hman thin  'Green MJA Day'  chu  vawiin  khan Lawngtlai bawrhsap Pu Donny Lalruatsanga hova hman a ni a. Hemi ni pual hian Lawngtlai Deputy Commissioner Bungalow  Chawnhuah  Thingtiak an phun a ni.

Green MJA Day hmanpuitu Lawngtlai Bawrhsap leh  MJA Lawngtlai District Patron ni bawk Pu Donny Lalruatsanga chuan Green MJA Day chibai bukna a nei a. MJA-  ten Thing tiak phuna ramngaw siam tura tha an thawh thinna chu chhuanawm a tih thu tarlangin midante tan entawntlak tak an ni a ti a.   Thingphuna ramngaw nuam leh ṭha zawk siama humhalh  zel chu thil pawimawh leh hlu tak a nih thu  sawiin,  a bik takin Green Mizoram Day  leh Green LADC Day -  a thingphunte humhalh leh enkawltu nih chu tihmakmawh a ni, a ti. Bawrhsap chuan  Environment humhalhna kawngah MJA-  te chu theihtawp chhuah tur leh mipuite zirtirtu ni zel turin duhsakna a hlan nghal bawk.

Green MJA Day puala Thingphun neih hma inkhawmna tawi neih hi MJA Lawngtlai District Vice President Pu H. Lalrinmawia'n a kaihruai a ni.

Green MJA Day-  hi Mizorama District hrang hrang zingah Lawngtlai District MJA - te  hi a  hmang thin awmchhun niin, kumtinin Thingtiak phun leh an phun tawhsa thawmṭhat leh enkawlzui nan an hmang thin a ni.

Attachment

title: Lungleiah Bawrhsap hovin Theiriat T-Way Filling Station bula lei min vanga harsatna chin fel dan tur sawi ho a ni
content:
1070/2026-2027

Lunglei, 2026 Nikir thla ni 18:Vawiin khan tun fur ruah sur avanga Lunglei Theiriat T-Way Filling Station bula lei minin National Highway kawng a hnawh pin ṭhin avang leh a chhak Theiriat kawng chung kawng bal avanga inkal pawhna chungchanga harsatna thleng mek leh a chin fel dan tur sawi hovin Lunglei District Bawrhsap Pi Navneet Mann hovin a hna thawh pindanah Lunglei District Disaster Management Authority (DDMA) member-te, a kaihhnawih sawrkar official-te, NHIDCL leh BRN Infrastructures Pvt Ltd ai awhte, Theiriat Local Council leh Young Mizo Association (YMA) Sub-Headquarters Lunglei ai awhte an ṭhu khawm.

Bawrhsap Pi Navneet Mann chuan Theiriat T-Way Filling Station bula National Highway kawng laih avanga fur ruahtuiin lei min a siam ṭhin leh mipuite'n harsatna an tawk ṭhin chu pawi a tih thu sawiin, chhiatna lian tham zawk a thlen loh hram a beisei thu a sawi a. Tuna lei minna hmun chu kum hmasa lama a lo min tawhna leh lei min hlauhthawnawmna hmun a nih avangin District thuneitute leh DDMA aṭangin a venna hna chak zawka thawk turin BRNIPL lam an nawr reng thu te, NHIDCL Executive Director leh hotu sang zawkah te pawh lehkha an ziah thu leh anni pawhin an bawhzuipui thu te sawiin, an duh anga company lamin hna an thawk chak tawk lo chu pawi a tih thu a sawi a. Fur ruahtui a tlak ṭan tawh avangin leh chhim lam mipuite'n harsatna an tawh mek avangin harsatna chin fel dan ngaihtuah thuai a ṭul thu sawiin, tu mah indem leh inmawhpuh lovin, harsatna nasa zawk a thlen lohna tur leh a chin felna turin thawh ho leh ṭan rual a pawimawh thu a sawi.

He hunah hian Assistant Commissioner (Revenue) Pi Jennifer Vanlalhruaii Pachuau hnen aṭangin DC hova kawng laih leh siam ṭhat hna thawh kal mek endik leh chak zawka hna thawh a nih theih nana a hmuna tlawh leh lehkha ziak hmanga hma an lak dan hrang hrang ngaihthlak a ni a. YMA Sub-Headquarters Lunglei President Pu K. Lalfakzuala chuan Theiriat lei min vanga harsatna thleng a hmuna an tlawh a, an hmuh dan sawiin, Theiriat Local Council Chairman Pu S. Sanglura chuan an harsatna leh hma lakna hrang hrang a tar lang bawk a. PWD, P&E leh PHED hotute'n inkal pawhna kawng, eng pekna leh tui pekna chungchanga harsatna awm leh a chin fel nana mamawh leh rawtnate an tar lang bawk.

Theiriat T-Way Filling Station bul hi fur ruah sur avangin June ni 15, 2026 (Thawhṭanni) zan khan minin, lei min hian kawng a hnawh bakah lirthei tang a awm bawk a. Lei min thu hriat a nih rualin lirthei tang leh harsatna tawkte ṭanpui turin DDMA leh Police lamin hma la nghalin, Theiriat Local Council leh YMA leh mi tlawmngaite nen lirthei tang hi lak chhuah a nih hnuah kawng hi khar rih a ni a. June ni 16, 2026 (Thawhlehni) zingkarah kawng hi then faia tihtlang niin, ruah sur vangin tlai lamah ping lehin, zankhuaa khar ngaiin a awm leh a. Kawng hi then fai leh ni mah se, ruah a sur reng avangin leh a khat tawka lei tla a awm leh ṭhin avangin lirthei leh mihring veivak tan a him lo hle a; a helna kawng a awm loh avangin he lai pal tlang ngai lirthei leh mipuite'n harsatna an tawk nasa hle a ni.

PWD hotute chuan harsatna nasa zawk pumpelhna turin kawng chung PWD Approach Road chu siam ṭha tura hma an lak mek thu sawiin, Approach Road siam nan hian cheng nuai 25 dah a nih thu an sawi a. Vawiin lamah a kawng chhuat siam rem hna thawh ṭan a nih thu leh hmanraw mamawh lak khawm mek a nih thu sawiin, a kawng a chhukchhoh deuh avangin leh fur ruah tui a tlak tawh avangin PCC pavement-a siam chuan duhthu a sam dawn loh avangin alkatra luan (flexible pavement)-a siam tum a nih thu an sawi a. He lai kawng siam chungchangah hian mahni theihna zawn ṭheuhvah theih tawp chhuah ho ṭha tih niin, PWD, Local Council leh YMA te inrawn tlangin hma lak ni thei se, tih a ni a. PWD te'n kawng siamnaa an hman atan hian BRNIPL chuan GSB cubic metre 100 thawhin, truck 4 hmantir se, tih a ni.

BRNIPL lamin National Highway kawnga leivung chu a hma lamin paih fai se tih niin, leivung paihna hmun tur remchang chu Local Council lamin lo ngaihtuah se, tih a ni a. Lei min belh zel tur ven nan Theiriat Local Council leh YMA kutah DDMA aṭangin silpaulin leh chhawmdawlna sum pek ni se, tih a ni a. Police lamin he lai hmuna duty an dah ṭhin chu a ṭul anga dah chhunzawm zel ni se tih niin, he lai kawngah hian harsatna nasa zawk a thlen lohna turin DDMA aṭangin uluk taka vil leh en zui zel ni se, tih a ni.

Attachment

title: SERCHCHIP-AH GOVERNOR’S FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE SEM A NI
content:
1069/2026-2027

Dated Serchhip, the 18thJune 2026:Vawiin khan Serchhip District Deputy Commissioner Pu Paul L. Khuma chuan Serchhip District chhunga Orphanage Home leh Home hrang hrang 5-te hnenah ṭanpuina sum ‘Governor’s Financial Assistance’ Rs. 35,000/- a sem

Tanpuina sumfai dawngtu Orphanages/Homes leh an sum dawn dante chu hengte hi an ni:

1. Old Age Home - Rs. 5,000/-

2. Zoar Urban Deprived - Rs. 5,000/-

3. Unity Orphanage Home - Rs. 5,000/-

4. Adult Rehabilitation Centre - Rs. 5,000/-

5. Shalom Special School - Rs. 15,000/-

Serchhip Bawrhsap Pu Paul L. Khuma chuan lawmthu sawiin, ṭanpuina sum dawngtute chu an an sum dawnte hlawk tak leh tha taka hmang turin duhsakna a hlan a ni.

title: SERCHHIP DISTRICT ELECTION OFFICER HOVIN ENUMERATION FORM 100% DIGITIZATION CHUNGCHANGAH PRESS MEET NEIH A NI
content:
1068/2026-2027

Dated Serchhip, the 18thJune 2026:Vawiin khan Serchhip District Election Officer Pu Paul L.Khuma chuan Press Conference kovin Special Intensive Revision (SIR) 2026 kalpui mekah Serchhip District chhunga Enumeration Form Digitization hna thawh chu June 17, 2026 zan khan 100% tluang taka zawh fel a ni thu a sawi.

Serchhip District Election Officer (DEO) Pu Paul L. Khuma chuan Special Intensive Revision neih mekah Serchhip District chu Mizorama Enumeration Form sem zo hmasa ber an nih thu sawiin Digitization report District- te’n an thehluh hmasak ber tum, June ni 8 – ah khan Serchhip District chu 6-na an nih thu a sawi a, ngawrh taka hna thawh zel niin, ECI-in June ni 28-a zawh tura a ruahman chu nizan dar 8:16 p.m. khan zawh fel niin, portal-ah zan dar 10-ah reflect a nih thu a sawi a; Champhai District nen June 17 zan khan zawh rual a nih thu sawiin, Assembly Constituency pakhat awmna Khawzawl District chuan June 16 khan an lo zawh tawh thu a sawi bawk.

SIR 2026 kalpui mek a hlawhtlin theih nana a sur a sa hnuaia thawktu hrang hrang - Election Branch-a thawkte, ERO, AERO, BLO Supervisor, BLO, BLA leh Media-te bakah kohhran, khawtlang hruaitu leh tlawmngai pawlte SIR buaipuitu zawng zawngte chungah lawmthu a sawi a ni.

Vawiin meeting-ah hian Election Officer Pi Lalchhanhimi’n Uncollectable chungchang leh thil pawimawh dangte sawizauna a nei bawk a. Mitthi tawh (deceased) te, chanchin chhuizui theih tawh loh (untraceable/absent) te, hmun danga pem hlen tawh (permanently shifted) te, hming chuang nawn (duplicate) leh Enumeration Form-a inziaklut duh lo (refused to sign EF) te chu Uncollectable tih niin Serchhip district-ah tun dinhmunah chutiang mi chu mi 1184 an awm thu a sawi a, July ni 4-ah Draft Roll tihchhuah tur niin, August ni 4 thlengin Draft Roll a sawisel duh nei leh dilna hrang hrang thlen duhte tan hun hawn a ni ang a, heng dilna te hi September ni 2, 2026 thlengin chinfelna hun hawn niin September ni 6, 2026 ah Final Roll tarchhuah a nih tur thu a sawi bawk.

Serchhip District-ah Enumeration Form hi 26th May, 2026 khan BLO te hnenah sem tan a ni a, 30th May, 2026 atangin BLO ten mipui (voter) hnenah an sem chhuak tan a.  Chief Election Office lam atanga 6th June-a sem zawh tura tih chu 4th June, 2026 ah 100% mipui hnenah sem zawh a ni a. Mipuite, anmahni inah kalchilh zel niin form fill up, signature pek leh BLO in a sign hnan tawh EF-te chu digitize zel an ni.

Vawiina hun hmannaah hian Pi Rebecca Laldinmawii, SDO(S) & ERO Tuikum (AC), Pi Lalchhanhimi, EO, Pu Chawngthu Lalrinhlua, AERO 27-Tuikum (AC), Pu Vanthangpuia, AERO 26-Serchhip (AC), Pi Lalchhanhimi, SDC leh Pu R. Vanneihtluanga, DIPRO te bakah Serchhip tualchhung chanchinbumite an tel a ni.

Attachment

title: MARALAND THEOLOGICAL COLLEGE-IN LAWMTHU SAWINA HUN AN HMANG
content:
1067/2026-2027

Siaha, 18th June 2026:Evangelical Church of Maraland (ECM) enkawl Maraland Theological College (MTC), Siaha chuan vawiin khan Senate of Serampore College (University) ah full affiliation an nih lawmna leh Pathian hnena lawmthu sawina hun Upa LC Hlychho Memorial Chapel, ECM Vaih, Siaha-ah an hmang.

Vawiin hun hmannaah hian Evangelical Church of Maraland hruaitute bakah Siaha khawpui chhunga kohhran hrang hrang hruaitute a tel. Rev. Chhuakhu Khithie, ES i/c Education, MTC chuan hun hman hi a kaihruai a, Rev. K. Laichhua Azyu leh Upa Pu M. Laikaw, CEM, MADC ten thuchah an sawi a, MTC Principal Rev. Dr. Setha Solo hnen aṭangin report ngaithlak a ni. Rev. Henry C. Lalenkawla, Vice Principal, MTC-in lawmthu sawina a nei a, malsawm ṭawngṭaina Rev. F. Vanlaldika, Moderator Elect. ECM Assembly in a nei a, hun hman tihtawp a ni.

Maraland Theological College hi October ni 7, 2017 ah din niin, kuminah Senate of Serampore College (University) pawmpuina an hmu a ni. Course pali - Bachelor of Theology, Bachelor of Christian Studies, Bachelor of Music (Church Music) leh Certificate of Theology zir theih anih bakah Missionary te tán training ṭha tâk pek ṭhin a ni. MTC ah hian tun dinhmun a Core Faculty sawm leh Guest Faculty paruk an awm mek a, non-teaching staff paruk bakah zirlai 45 an awm mêk a ni.

title: TRANSPORT DEPARTMENT THUCHHUAK
content:
1066/2026-2027

Aizawl the 18th June, 2026:Tunlai hian thil thawn/Bungraw thawn chungchangah fimkhur a ngaih avangin leh a neitu/lo dawngtu tur hriatchian a ngaih thin avangin tun achinah chuan Bus-a Bungraw thawn tur reng reng chuan aDawngtu tur Hming, Address leh Phone Numberchiang taka ziah lan tur a ni a,a Thawntu Hming, Address leh Phone Number chiang taka ziah lan bawk tur a ni.

Hetianga a thawntu leh dawngtu tur nihna chiang taka ziah lan awmlo chu Driver leh Conductor ten phurh tawh loh tur a ni.

Lo phur lui an awm anih chuan harsatna an tawh pawhin Department in a buaipui lo ang.

title: AMC THUCHHUAK/ LEI LAIH/LEI VUNG HMANGA KHUAR HNAWH KHAP
content:
1065/2026-2027

Aizawl the 18th June, 2026:Aizawl khawpui chhungah fur laia leimin avangin chhiatna hrang hrang a lo thleng tawh thin a. Chenna in leh lo leh bungrua hlu tak tak channa bakah mihring tawrhna-hliam tuar leh nunna channa hial a lo thleng tawh thin a.

Chuvangin, The Aizawl Municipal Corporation (Site Development & Slope Modification) Regulations, 2017 regulation no. 10 in fur ruahtui tam laia hmunhma siamremna tura leilaih leh a kaihhnawih te khap tura Aizawl Municipal Corporation thuneihna a tarlan angin1stJuly - 31stOctober, 2026 chhungin Aizawl khawpui, AMC huamchinah lei laih, leivung hmanga khuar hnawh leh a kaihhnawihte khap a ni.He khapna bawhchhiate chu Mizoram Municipalities Act, 2007 Section 137, 374 leh 386 hmangin hrem theih an ni ang.

He khapna kenkawh chhung hian chhiatna lian zawk a thlen lohna tura tul bik thilah phalna dil theih a ni ang. Hetiang tul bik thilah chuan a hmunhma dinhmun chiang taka tarlangin Aizawl Municipal Corporation-ah dilna thehluh theih a ni ang a; dilnate hi a hmunah uluk taka enfiah tur niin phalsak ngei ngei tur tihna a ni lovang.

Lei laih khap hian thlan laih te, chhiatna thleng avanga leimin senghawi leh thenfai tula chetna te, tul bik thil avanga phalna pek te chu a huam lovang.

title: STATE TASK FORCE FOR IMMUNIZATION THUKHAWM
content:
1064/2026-2027

Aizawl June ni 18, 2016:Vawiin khan State Task Force for Immunization chu SAD Second Floor Hall-I ah H&FW Commissioner & Secretary Pi K. Lalrinzuali hovin an thukhawm.

He meeting-ah hian July ni 1-a Mizorama National Immunization Day hman tur atana hri danna pek (immunization) kalpui dan leh, kumin chhunga hmalakna te thlirho a ni a. Polio bikah chuan India ram chu 2014 khan Polio um bo tawhna ram a puan ni tawh mahse, Polio natna la awmna ram Pakistan te, Myanmar leh Bangladesh ten an hual avangin immunization programme hi la thlah theih a nih loh thu sawi a ni.

National Immunization Day atan hian tun dinhmunah Mizoram chhungah chenna in 268,925 tuam tawh niin, naupang kum 5 hnuai lam mi 1,02,823 an tel a ni. HPV Vaccine chungchang report-ah a lan danin 17.06.2026 thlengin Mizoramah he HPV hi mi 10,351 hnenah pek tum (target) chu 99% pek zawh tawh a ni a. Serchhip, Lawngtlai leh Siaha district ah coverage a hniam thu tarlan a ni. Vaccine coverage harsatna hrang hrang te sawilan niin, fur ruahtui tam avanga inkalpawh harsat avfang te, thu belhchian dawllo thehdarh avanga vaccine hlauh tak avang leh chhan dang hrang hrang vang a nih thu an sawi.

Meeting-ah hian district hrang hranga Immunization kalpui dan report Dr. Zoramruati Ralte, Joint Director (EPI), State Immunization Officer chuan presentation a pe a. Dr. Priscilla V, UNICEF Health Consultant chuan Lawngtlai District chhunga Zero Dose coverage chungchang a sawi a. UNDP Senior Programme Officer HPV Coverage chungchang a sawi bawk.

title: KHAWZAWLA PLUCK AND PAY FESTIVAL HMAN DAN TUR RELIN ORGANISING COMMITTEE THUKHAWM
content:
1062/2026-2027

Dated Khawzawl, the 18th June, 2026 :Vawiin khan Khawzawlah Pluck and Pay Festival hman dan tur relin Organizing Committee chu Addl DC Pu K. Zorammuana kaihhruaiin Video Conference Room, DC Office-ah an thukhawm.Vawiin thutkhawmah hian Pluck and Pay Festival hman dan tur programme kimchang taka rel a ni a. Event manage-tu tur thlan a nih bakah Line Department leh VCP-te hnenah mawhphurhna hrang hrang inpek a ni bawk a. July 28-30, 2026 chhungin Khawzawl district chhunga Chawngtlai leh Tualte khuaahte neih tur a ni a. Heng khuaahte hian thei leh thlai huanahte a duh apiang tan thei leh thlai rahte lawh theih a ni dawn a, kuthnathawktute thar chhuah thlai leh thei leh thil dangte phochhuahna tur stall hrang hrang siam a ni dawn bawk a. He festival-ah hian concert buatsaih a ni dawn a, zaithiam lar tak takte leh khaw chhunga zaithiamten khualzinte zaiin an awi dawn a ni.Pluck and Pay Festival hi Tourism Department hmalaknaa buatsaih tur niin, Khawzawl district chhunga khualzin hipna leh agro-tourism tihlarna tura ruahman a ni a. He festival thil tum ber chu Khawzawl district chhunga thei leh thlai chi hrang hrang Chawngtlai sapthei, Tualte tomato leh adangte tihlara, kuthnathawktuten awlsam zawka an thil tharte an hralh theih nan leh an dinhmun chawikan nan a ni.Vawiin thutkhawmah hian Organising Committee member thenkhat office-ah thukhawmin, a hmuna kal remchang loten Video Conference-in an zawm a ni.

title: Mamit-ah ALIMCO hnuaia scheme kaltlangin rualbanlo leh kum upate tan mamawh sem a ni
content:
1062/2026-2027

Dated: Mamit the 18th of June,2026:Vawiin khan DC Conference, Mamit-ah Artificial Limbs Manufacturing Corporation of India (ALIMCO) hnuaia scheme kaltlangin rualbanlo leh kum upate tan mamawh (distribution of aids and appliances) semna hun hman a ni a, Pu Suvigya S Chandra, District Bawrhsap chuan rualbanlo leh kum upate hnenah a sem  chhuak.

Vawiin hun hi Pu Lalnunfela Chawngthu, SDO (Sadar) chuan kaihruaiin Sawrkar hmalakna chu lawmawm a tih thu a sawia, mamawh te tan quality tha leh a thlawna dawn theihna Sawrkar in a ruahhman chu dik tak leh hlawk taka hmang turin a chah a ni.

Pu F Zorinsanga, ALIMCO represwngatuve chuan ALIMCO sawi zauna hun a hmang a, ALIMCO hnuaiah hian item hrang hrang wheelchair, hearing aid, walking stick leh a dangte mamawh dan a zirin a thlawna sem chhuah a ni a, rualbanlo leh kum 60 aia upa ten dilna an thehlut thei a ni.

Vawiin hun hmanna ah hian  Addl DC, ALIMCO hotute bakah beneficiaries te an tel  a ni.

Attachment

title: CHIEF MINISTER CHAIRS THIRD Mi-PRAGATI MEETING
content:
185/2026-2027

Aizawl, 18 June 2026:Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma today chaired the third meeting of the Mizoram Pro-Active Governance and Timely Implementation (Mi-PRAGATI) at the Chief Minister's Conference Hall.

Mi-PRAGATI has been established to monitor major developmental projects across the State, address implementation challenges, strengthen inter-departmental coordination, and ensure the timely and effective execution of government programmes. The committee serves as a high-level mechanism for expediting government works and improving overall governance.

During the meeting, a review was conducted of 53 Government of Mizoram projects funded under the NABARD Rural Infrastructure Development Fund (RIDF), each with a project cost exceeding ₹3 crore. Progress of the projects was assessed, reasons for delays in certain cases were examined, and measures for accelerating implementation were discussed.

Officials of the Railways also presented details of the proposed Sairang–Hmawngbuchhuah Railway Project, which is estimatec to cost around ₹44,800 crore. The proposed railway line will span about 224 kilometres and include 20 stations. The Chief Minister had earlier advised that the proposed Thenzawl Railway Station be located closer to Thenzawl town, as the initially identified site was situated at a considerable distance. During the meeting, Railway officials presented their proposal for relocating the station to a more convenient location.

The meeting also reviewed the progress of the Ropuiliani Tribal Freedom Fighter Museum, the State Tribal Institute, implementation of PM SHRI initiatives, Swachh Bharat Mission activities, the Kaladan Multi-Modal Transit Transport Project, and major projects being executed under the Public Works Department (PWD). Challenges affecting project implementation and projects requiring close monitoring were discussed, and measures for resolving the issues were deliberated.

Attachment

title: CHIEF MINISTER HOVIN Mi PRAGATI MEETING NEIH A NI
content:
1061/2026-2027

Aizawl the 18th June 2026:Vawiin khan Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma kaihhruaiin Mizoram Pro-Active Governance and Timely Implementation (Mi-PRAGATI) meeting vawi thumna CM Conference Hall-ah neih a ni.

Mi-PRAGATI hi state chhunga hmasawnna hnathawh lian zualte vil a, harsatna an tawhte chin fel dan dappuitu leh a tul anga department hrang hrang thawhhona tichak tura din a ni a. Sorkar hnathawhte langtlang leh rang zawka kalpui zel dan ngaihtuahtu committee thunei tak a ni.

Vawiin meetingah hian:

NABARD RIDF sum hmanga Mizoram sorkar hnathawh mek, Project amount Rs. 3 Crore aia tam chin project 53 te ennawnna (review) neih a ni a. An zinga hna kal muang zualte kal muan chhan tarlan a ni a. Chak zawka hnathawh dan tur pawh sawi a ni.

Rs. 44,800 Crore vel senga thawh tur Sairang - Hmawngbuchhuah rel kawng ruahmanna Railways lamin an sawifiah a. He rel kawng hi kilometer 224 vela thui, station 20 awm tura duan a ni. Thenzawl Rel Station awmna tura riruan tawh chu Thenzawl khua atanga a hlat deuh avangin Chief Minister hian hnai deuh zawka dah dan zawng turin a lo hrilh tawh a. Vawiin hian hnai deuh zawka dah an tum dan an tarlang a ni.

Ropuiliani Tribal Freedom Fighter Museum sak mek chungchang te, State Tribal Institute sak mek te, PM SHRI hmalakna kal mek te, Swachh Bharat hmalakna te, Kaladan Multi Modal Transit Transport Project hmalakna te, PWD hnuaia hnathawh lian zualho, hnathawh timuang thei harsatna an tawh mekte leh project kalpui lai han enzui ngai bikte an sawiho bawk a. An harsatnate chinfel dan tur an sawiho nghal zel bawk.

Meeting-ah hian Pu K. Sapdanga, Home Minister, Pu Lalthansanga, EF & CC Minister, Pu B. Lalchhanzova, FCS & CA Minister, Prof Lalnilawma, PHE Minister bakah Chief Secretary Pu Khilli Ram Meena leh official pawimawh te, Mizoram chhunga project lian thawktu central agency te an tel a. Online-in Ministry of External Affairs aiawh te, NHIDCL Managing Director te, district Bawrhsap ten an zawm bawk.

Attachment

title: Mizoram Governor Graces Investiture Ceremony at St. Paul’s Higher Secondary School
content:
184/2026-2027

Aizawl, the 18th June, 2026:Mizoram Governor, General (Dr) Vijay Kumar Singh, PVSM, AVSM, YSM (Retd), graced the Investiture Ceremony 2026 at St. Paul’s Higher Secondary School, Montfort Hills, Tlangnuam, Aizawl, as Chief Guest today. The event formally inducted the newly elected school leaders for the academic year 2026-27.

During the ceremony, the Governor presented badges to the 20 newly elected school leaders and handed over house flags to the respective House Captains of the five houses - Louise House, Montfort House, Gabriel House, Francis House and Abraham House.

In his address, the Governor General (Dr) Vijay Kumar Singh congratulated the newly elected leaders and urged them to lead with sincerity, humility and dedication. “True leadership is not about commanding others, it is about serving others. A good leader listens, understands and inspires by setting an exemplary life,” he said. He emphasised that leadership should be measured not by the privileges one enjoys, but by the service one renders. The Governor lauded St. Paul’s Higher Secondary School for its balanced emphasis on academic excellence and character building. “Education must prepare young people not only for successful careers but also for responsible citizenship. Our country needs honest, capable youth who are committed to the welfare of society,” he added.

Describing the Investiture Ceremony as more than a formal event, the Governor called it a celebration of leadership, responsibility and trust. He reminded the students of the duties that accompany leadership and encouraged them to develop strong personal qualities during their school years. He further advised the students that the knowledge and skills they acquire now will shape their future contributions to society and the nation. Noting the challenges of a rapidly advancing technological world and increasing global interconnectedness, the Governor urged them to remain adaptable, well-informed and morally grounded. Lauding the well-conducted and beautifully organised ceremony, the Governor expressed strong confidence in the students and wished them success in their future endeavours.

Earlier in the programme, Brother Kuriyan M.S., Principal of St. Paul’s Higher Secondary School, delivered the welcome address. The Guest of Honour, Mr. David H. Lalthangliana, AIG (Hqrs), PHQ, examined the House Captains-elect from all five houses. The Principal then administered the oath of office to the newly elected school leaders, followed by acceptance speeches from the House Captains.

Attachment

title: Mizoram Larsap-in St. Paul's Higher Secondary School Investiture Ceremony 2026 a hmanpui
content:
1060/2026-2027

Aizawl, the 18th June, 2026:Mizoram Larsap, General (Dr) Vijay Kumar Singh, PVSM, AVSM, YSM (Retd) chuan vawiin khan St. Paul's Higher Secondary School, Montfort Hills, Tlangnuam, Aizawlin kum 2026-2027 chhunga zirlai hruaitu tur House Captain thlan thar 20 te lakluhna "Investiture Ceremony 2026" a buatsaih chu Khuallian niin a hmanpui. Larsap hian house hrang hrang 5 - Louise House, Montfort House, Gabriel House, Francis House leh Abraham House hruaitu thlan tharte badge a bel sak bakah house flag a hlana, fuihna thu a sawi bawk.

Larsap General (Dr) Vijay Kumar Singh chuan hruaitu thlan tharte duhsakna hlanin, anmahni thlanga an chunga rinna nghattute beiseina tihpuiltling turin taimakna, thuhnuairawlhna leh inpekna nena hna thawk turin a fuih a. Hruaitu dik tak nihna chu midangte hnena thu pekah nilo, rawngbawl sakah a innghat zawk a, hruaitu tha chuan ngun taka ngaithlain thil nih dan a hre thiam zung zung a, entawntlak taka nungin mite a hneh thin a ni, a ti. Midangte nena intawiawm tawn tura fuihin, hruaitu dinhmun chu hamthatna dawnah ni lovin midang tana pek tamah teh thin a ni, a ti a. Zirna ngawr ngawrah insawrbing lova mihring puitling chher chhuah kawnga St. Paul's Higher Secondary School hmalakna fakawm a tih thu sawiin, zirna chuan eizawnna kawnga hlawhtling tura thalaite a hrual mai piah lamah khua leh tui tha ni tura a chher hriam tel a ngaih thu a sawi a. Kan ramin thalai rinawm leh chhawrtlak, khawtlang hmasawn nana thahnemngai a mamawh thu a sawi bawk.

Larsap chuan Investiture Ceremony chu hruaitu nihna dinhmun mai bakah a kentel mawhphurhna leh midangin rinna an nghahte hlen chhuak tura bultanna a nih thu a sawi a. Zirlaite chu an nun uluk tur leh zirlai an nih chhunga hmasawn tura inzir nasa tura fuihin, tuna in thil zir leh hriatte hian in dam chhung a awh tawh dawn bakah khawtlang leh ram tana in theihna lan chhuah thlengin a pui dawn che u a ni, a ti. Thiamna sang zel leh chanchin inhriatpawh tawn zung zung theihna khawvela cheng mek an nih avangin zirlaite chu insiamrem thiam turte, inzir peih tur leh chhia leh tha hriatnate fimkhur taka hmang thin turin a chah a. Investiture ceremony hmang tura sawm a ni chu lawmawm tiin, programme felfai a tih thu leh entawntlak a tih thu a sawi a. Zirlaiteah beiseina sang tak a neih thu pawh sawiin, an kalzelna tura duhsakna a hlan a ni.

He hunah hian Brother Kuriyan M.S., Principal, St. Paul's Higher Secondary School chuan inlawmna thu a sawi a. Khualzahawm Pu David H. Lalthangliana, AIG (Hqrs), PHQ chuan House Captain thlan tharte endikna hun a hmang a. Principal kaihhruaia thu tiam an lak hnuah House Captain-te atangin thusawi ngaihthlak a ni bawk.

Attachment

title: COOPERATION MINISTER ATTENDS 37th ANNUAL GENERAL ASSEMBLY OF MIZORAM STATE COOPERATIVE UNION
content:
183/2026-2027

Aizawl, 18 June 2026:Minister for Cooperation, Pu P.C. Vanlalruata, today attended the 37th Annual General Assembly of the Mizoram State Cooperative Union (MSCU) held at Vanapa Hall as the Chief Guest.

Addressing the gathering, the Minister expressed his appreciation for being invited to the 37th Annual General Assembly of MSCU. He stated that cooperative societies play a vital role in transforming Mizoram and strengthening the State’s economy. He informed the Assembly that the Government of Mizoram has already submitted a proposal to the Ministry of Cooperation under the Mizoram Cooperative Joint Mission for the establishment of at least 1,000 profit-making cooperative societies. It is envisaged that each society would achieve an annual turnover of at least ₹1 crore. He further stated that successful implementation of the mission could potentially increase members’ incomes by 100 per cent. A proposal amounting to ₹303 crore for implementation of the mission over a period of three years has already been submitted. The Minister expressed confidence that the mission would usher in a new era of growth and prosperity for cooperative societies in Mizoram.

Pu P.C. Vanlalruata also highlighted the State Government’s efforts to support the Government of India’s White Revolution 2.0 initiative. In this regard, the Government of Mizoram has facilitated the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) between the Mizoram Milk Producers’ Cooperative Union Limited (MULCO) and the National Dairy Development Board (NDDB). Through this partnership, it is expected that at least 1,000 high-yielding cattle will be inducted over the next three years, enabling MULCO to increase its milk production to over 15,000 litres per day. He further informed that an outlay of ₹24.10 crore (₹16.78 crore from the Government of India and ₹7.32 crore from the Government of Mizoram) has been earmarked under the MULCO Revitalization Initiative.

The Minister also announced that a project worth ₹5 crore under the Silk Samagra 2.0 Scheme for the Integrated Muga Silk Development Project has already been approved. In addition, a proposal amounting to ₹125 crore has been submitted for the establishment of an Integrated Sericulture Post-Processing Hub.

On the occasion, Commissioner & Secretary, Cooperation Department, Pu Udit Prakash Rai, attended as Guest of Honour and addressed the gathering. He highlighted the Cooperation Department’s collaborative efforts with various government departments and described the achievement of Mizoram Cooperative Apex Bank (MCAB) in obtaining Scheduled Bank Status as a significant milestone for the cooperative movement in Mizoram. He further stated that cooperative societies are expected to contribute 10 per cent of Mizoram’s Gross Domestic Product (GDP) by the year 2047.

Registrar of Cooperative Societies, Pu John L.T. Sanga, also addressed the Assembly. He emphasized the Department’s commitment to supporting entrepreneurs and strengthening cooperative societies across the State.

Mizoram State Cooperative Union (MSCU), established in 1975, is the oldest State-level cooperative society in Mizoram. It was formed with the objective of providing education and training to cooperative societies and promoting awareness and understanding of the cooperative movement among the people of Mizoram.

The programme was chaired by MSCU President, Pu K. Lalchamreia. MSCU Chief Executive Officer (CEO), Pu K. Lalruatfela, presented the Annual Report for 2025–2026, while MSCU Vice-Chairman, Pu F. Lalchungnunga, delivered the vote of thanks.

Attachment

title: CHIEF MINISTER FLAGS OFF 219 NEW POLICE VEHICLES PROCURED UNDER ASUMP
content:
182/2026-2027

Aizawl, 18 June 2026:Chief Minister Shri Lalduhoma today flagged off 219 new police vehicles procured under the Assistance to States & UTs for Modernisation of Police (ASUMP) Scheme at the 3rd Battalion MAP Parade Ground, Mualpui. Home Minister Pu K. Sapdanga and senior police officials attended the programme.

A total of 219 vehicles have been procured under the scheme, with some yet to arrive. Every Police Station in Mizoram (44 in total) will receive at least one new vehicle, 15 outposts will benefit re-allocation. And new vehicles shall also be alloted to Battalions, Specialised Units and Police Training Schools.

Addressing the gathering, the Chief Minister said that Mizoram has received Rs. 44.53 crore under the ASUMP Scheme, including the State's share, of which Rs. 30.45 crore has been utilised for vehicle procurement. The remaining funds have been used for the purchase of modern policing equipment and infrastructure.

He noted that vehicle procurement under the Police Modernisation Scheme had not been permitted since 2017, creating transportation challenges for the Police Department. Following sustained efforts by the State Government and Mizoram Police, particularly the office of the Director General of Police, approval was eventually secured. He also pointed out that Mizoram had been unable to avail benefits under the scheme since 2021 due to technical issues and described its restoration under his government as a significant achievement.

The Chief Minister urged recipients to maintain the vehicles properly so that they can serve the Police Department for many years.

Director General of Police Pu Sharad Agarwal, IPS, said that the initiative was aimed not only at acquiring new vehicles but also at strengthening the Police Force and enhancing its operational capability to meet the demands of modern policing.

Attachment

title: KOLASIB-AH DISTRICT TOURISM MANAGEMENT COMMITTEE (DTMC) THUKHAWM
content:
1059/2026-2027

Kolasib, 18th June, 2026:Vawiin khan DC Conference Hall, Kolasib-ah Kolasib Bawrhsap Pu H. Dolianbuaia hovin District Tourism Management Committee (DTMC) thutkhawm neih a ni.

Thutkhawm kaihruaitu DC chuan thu sawiin, district hmasawnna tur thil pawimawh hrang hrang ngaihtuahin thutkhawm neih fo thin ni mah se, Tourism (khualzin hip luh) lam hawia thutkhawm neih a ni chu lawmawm a tih thu a sawi a. Tun dinhmunah Kolasib district-a neih turin ruahmanna (festival) lian tham tak pahnih buatsaih tuma hmalak mek a nih thu sawiin, hotu lam atanga pawm a nih chuan khualzin hip theihna tur festival lian tham tak kan huaihawt thei dawn a ni, a ti.

"Kan district chhungah hian khualzinte tlawh tlak hmunhma tha tak tak kan nei a. Hengte hi pholanga, hma kan sawn chuan a hlawkpui ber tur chu mipuite bawk an ni," tiin, dawr leh eizawnna chi hrang hrang atanga sum thahnem tak mipuiten an lakluh theihna tur a nih avangin DTMC member kalkhawmte chu ngaihtuahna seng a, thahnemngai taka thawk turin a chah a ni.

He hunah hian Pi Rachel Lalrinhlui, Deputy Director, Tourism Department pawhin thu sawina hun hmangin, India ram pumah Mizoram chu khualzin tlawhtu pung chak ber (highest growth rate in tourist arrivals) kan nih mek thu a tarlang a. Kolasib District chhunga khualzin hip theihna hmunhma (tourist destinations) pawimawh zualte thlang chhuak a, engtianga tlangzarh leh tihhmasawn zel tur nge tih chu District level-a ngun taka ngaihtuah ho a pawimawh hle a ni, a ti. Khawtlang mipuite nena thawhhona (community participation) leh mipuite zirtirna (awareness) uar taka kalpui zel a ngaih thu a sawi bawk.

Meeting-ah hian kum 2025-26 chhunga Kolasib District-a khualzin tlawhtu dinhmun (statistics) thlirho a ni a. Report-a a lan danin, he hun chhung hian Kolasib-ah khualzin 90,533 an awm a, heng zingah hian ram dang mi (foreign tourists) 35 an tel a ni. Hei hi Mizoram puma khualzin tlawhtu zawng zawng atanga chhutin za zela 9.24 (9.24%) zet a tling a ni.

Hei bakah hian Kolasib District chhunga Tourist Destination lian tham zual awmte thlirhovin, heng hmunte tihhmasawn dan tur leh tlawhtu tam zawk an awm theih dan turte chipchiar taka sawiho nghal a ni.

Attachment

title: CHIEF MINISTER-IN ASUMP ATANGA DAWN POLICE LIRTHEI THAR 219 VAILIAM
content:
1058/2026-2027

Aizawl the 18th June 2026:Vawiin khan Mizoram Police Headquarters (PHQ) chuan 3rd Battalion MAP Parade Ground, Mualpuiah Assistance to State & UTs for Modernisation of Police Scheme atanga lei lirthei chi hrang hrang 219 chu Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma'n a vailiam a, Pu K. Sapdanga, Home Minister-in a telpui.

Lirtheite hi an la rawn thleng kim lo deuh a, an thlen kim hunah chuan a vaiin 219 an ni ang. Mizorama Police Station zawng zawng (P/S 44) ten a thar pakhat tal an dawng vek dawn a, chu mi zarah chuan Outpost 15 pawhin lirthei an kawl theih phah dawn a ni. Battalion te, Specialised Unit te leh Police Training School te pawhin an dawng bawk ang.

Thusawiin Chief Minister chuan, Central sorkar Scheme, Assistance to State & UTs for Modernisation of Police, (ASUMP) atanga kan fund hmuh zawng zawng hi state share-te nen Rs. 44.53 Crore a ni a, heng zinga Rs. 30.45 hi lirthei lei nan bik hman a ni a, a dang hi Police thuam chakna hmanraw thar chi hrang hrang lei nana hman a ni.

Kum 2017 atang khan ASUMP atanga lirthei lam chi lei hi central sorkar hian a rawn khap a. Kan police ten lirthei lamah harsatna an tawk chho a. Assembly House lamahte pawh sawi rik a ni thin a.

Kan mamawhziate uar taka sawi chungin, nikum khan action plan kan thehlut a. Kan DGP Pu Sharad Agarwal hian theihtawp chhuahin a bawhzui a. Hun rei tak an lo phal tawh loh thil kha min rawn phalsak ta a ni.

Technical thila harsatna kan neih avangin Mizoram hian Police Modernisation Scheme hi kum 2021 atang khan kan dawng tawh lo va. Kan sorkarah kan han hmu thar leh ta hi a lawmawmin a chhinchhiahtlak a. Kan Home Minister leh a thawhpuite chungah hian kan lawm hle a ni, a ti.

Lirthei thar dawngtute chu an lirtheite duat tak enkawl tura chahin, a thar han lei leh hi a harsa a, vawiina kan dawnte hi hun rei tak hman theih kan tum dawn nia a ti.

Kar hmasaa New Delhi-a Pu Amit Shah, Union Home Minister nena an inkawmna chungchang pawh tarlangin, NE state dang zawng zawngten Security Related Expenditure an dawn laiin Mizoramin hian kan dawng ve lo va. Cyber Crime dona tur leh Drugs tawlhruk dona atana kan hman ve tur SRE Fund hi min pe ve turin ka dil a. PM DevINE lamah project rawn thenlut zawk la, ka lo bawhzuisak ang che min lo ti a. Tin, raltlante chhawmdawlna pawh ka dil leh a, chu pawh chu buhfai Rs. 10 Crore man hu min pek leh tur thu min hrilh a. Home Ministry hian kan harsatnate hi min hrethiam a, kan lawm thin hle a ni, a ti bawk.

Pu K. Sapdanga, Home Minister pawhin thusawiin, Mizoram police te thawhrimnaah lawmthu a sawi a, lirthei tharin an nitin hnaah awlsamna tam tak a thlen a beisei thu a tarlang. Kan police te thuam chak hi kan ram himna thuam chakna leh khawtlang nuam neihna a ni tih kan hria a. Thuam chak zel kan tum a ni, a ti.

Lirthei vailiam inkhawmah hian Pu Sharad Agarwal, IPS, DGP Mizoram pawhin thu a sawi a, lirthei thar neih duhna ngawr ngawra he hmalakna hi kalpui kan ni lo va. Hna thawh tha zawk kan neih theih nana inthuamchakna, tunlai changkanna umpha tura inbuatsaihna a ni, a ti. Tun hnaia sorkarin police hnaruak tam tham tak a hnawhkhahsak avangin lawmthu a sawi bawk.

ASUMP kaltlanga hma an lak dan tlangpui report Pu Vanlalfaka Ralte, MPS, AIG (Modernisation) chuan a sawi a ni.

Attachment

title: COOPERATION MINISTER-IN MIZORAM STATE COOPERATIVE UNION (MSCU) ANNUAL GENERAL ASSEMBLY VAWI 37-NA A HMANPUI
content:
1057/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, the 18th June 2026:Vawiin khan Cooperation Department changtu Minister Pu P.C. Vanlalruata chuan Mizoram State Cooperative Union (MSCU) Annual General Assembly vawi 37-na Vanapa Hall-ah an hmanna chu khuallian niin a telpui.

Cooperation Minister chuan MSCU inkhawmpuilian vawi 37-na a hmanpui thei chu lawmawm a tih thu sawiin, Cooperative Society-te chu Mizoram hmel tidanglama, state economy tithang theitu an ni, a ti a. Sawrkar pawhin cooperative society-te hmasawn zelna atan Mizoram Cooperative Joint Mission hnuaiah ‘profit-making cooperative society’ (hlawkna tel thei cooperative society) sangkhat (1000) tal din tura rawtna proposal Ministry of Cooperation-ah an thehluh tawh thu leh society tinte chuan annual turnover rate cheng vaibelchhe khat tal neih theih an inbeisei thu a sawi a. Hemi anga an hlawhtling thei a nih chuan member-te income pawh 100% a pun dawn thu sawiin, he mission-ah hian cheng vaibelchhe 303 lai kum 3 chhunga implement theihna tur proposal thehluh a ni tawh a. He mission hi a hlawhtling thei a nih chuan Mizoram society-te chuan hmasawnna rahbi thar an chuangkai ngei ang, tiin Pu P.C. Vanlalruata chuan a sawi.

Pu P.C Vanlalruata chuan central sawrkarin White Revolution 2.0 initiative (bawnghnute tharchhuah tihpunna) a kalpui mek bawhzuina atan Mizoram sawrkar chuan MULCO (Mizoram Milk Producers’ Cooperative Union Limited) leh NDDB (National Dairy Development Board) te chu MoU a sign pui tawh thu a sawi a. He inremna kaltlang hian bawngpui tha 1000 tal kum thum chhunga lakluh theih a, MULCO-in bawnghnute a tharchhuah pawh litre 15,000 chuanga a pun theih ngei beisei a ni, a ti a. He MULCO tihchangtlun zualna (revitalization) initiative-ah hian cheng vaibelchhe 24.10 (Central sawrkar tum: vbch. 16.78 leh Mizoram sawrkar tum: vbch. 7.32) ruahman a ni. Minister chuan Silk Samagra 2.0 scheme hnuaia ‘Integrated Muga Silk Development Project’ atan cheng vaibelchhe 5 approve a nih tawh thu leh, ‘Integrated Sericulture Post-processing Hub’ atan cheng vaibelchhe 125 lai budget atan ruahmanna siama thehluh a nih tawh thu a sawi bawk.

He programme-ah hian Cooperation Department Commissioner & Secretary Pu Udit Prakash Rai chu khual zahawm niin thu a sawi a. Cooperation department chuan department hrang hrangte nen thawhhona an neih thu te sawiin, Mizoram Cooperative Apex Bank (MCAB) in Scheduled Bank status a nei thei chu Mizoram cooperative society te tana mel lung pawimawh tak a nih thu a sawi a. Kum 2047-ah chuan Mizoram GDP 10% chu cooperative society-ten an thawhchhuah theih a beisei tih Pu Udit Prakash Rai chuan a sawi. Cooperative Society Registrar Pu John L.T. Sanga pawhin thu sawiin Cooperation Department chuan kuthnathawktute a ngaih pawimawh thu leh cooperative society-te tan nasa taka hmalak an tum thu a sawi bawk.

Mizoram State Cooperative Union (MSCU) hi Mizorama State Level Cooperative Society-te zinga upa ber niin kum 1975 khan din a ani a, Mizorama Cooperative Society zawng zawngte hnena zirtirna hrang hrang pe tura din a ni a, Mizoram mipui ten Cooperative Society an hriat bel a, an hman thiam theihna atan hma an la thin a ni.

Vawiin programme-ah hian MSCU President Pu K.Lalchamreia chuan chairman niin inkhawm hi a kaihruai a,MSCU Chief Executive Officer (CEO) Pu K.Lalruatfela chuan Annual Report (2025-26) pein, MSCU Vice-chairman Pu F.Lalchungnunga chuan lawmthu sawina neiin inkhawm hi a khar a ni.

Attachment

title: LAWNGTLAI BAWRHSAP-IN GILEAD DROP - IN  CENTRE LAWNGTLAI BAZAR TLAWH
content:
1056/2026-2027

Lawngtlai 18th June, 2026: Vawiin khan Lawngtlai bawrhsap Pu Donny Lalruatsanga chuan Gilead Drop- In  Centre, Lawngtlai Bazar chu a hmunah kalin a tlawh.

Pu Donny Lalruatsanga chuan Lawngtlai District-mipuite tana Gilead Drop-In Centre te hnathawh fakawm a tih thu tarlangin, hma lam hunah pawh thahnem ngai taka thawk turin a fuih a. HIV/AIDS-  do kawngah tha thlah theih loh a nih thu sawiin, a do kawngah tanho a ngai hle a, District Administration chuan theihtawp a chhuah zel tur thu a tarlang a ni. Ruihlo do kawngah Ruihhlo mamawhtu tih tlem chu thil pawimawh hmasa tak a ni tiin, hemi kawngah hian chak leh mumal zawka hmalak a tul hle a ti a. Lawngtlai District- ah HIV/AIDS a darh zel lohna turin,  sawrkar,NGO, Kohhran leh mipuite an pawimawh tak zel tih tarlangin, bawrhsap chuan thawkho zel turin a sawm nghal bawk.

Bawrhsap hi thawktute’n lo dawngsawngin, Office hmunhmate fanpuiin an hnathawh tlangpuite  lo hilhin,  mamawh leh qharsatna an neihte an lo thlen a. Pu Donny Lalruatsanga chuan  mamawh an neihte zingah Water Filter- chu pek an nih tur thu a sawi a. Mamawh dang  bakah an harsatna an neihte  chin fel pui tura hmalak a nih tur thu a sawi a.  HIV/AIDS kaihhnawiha hma lak dan turah  ruahmanna  kawng hrang hrangte an sawiho bawk  a ni.

Attachment

title: ENUMERATION FORM DIGITISATION COMPLETED IN SERCHHIP DISTRICT
content:
182/2026-2027

Dated Serchhip, 17th June 2026:Under the ongoing Special Intensive Revision (SIR) 2026, the digitisation of Enumeration Forms in Serchhip District was successfully completed late on 17 June 2026.

Serchhip District comprises three Assembly Constituencies—26-Serchhip (ST), 27-Tuikum (ST), and 28-Hrangturzo (ST)—with a total of 55,389 electors. Of these, 1,184 cases were identified as uncollectable, including deceased electors, untraceable or absent persons, permanently shifted voters, duplicate entries, and individuals who refused to sign the Enumeration Form (EF).

Beginning on 30 May 2026, Booth Level Officers (BLOs) undertook door-to-door distribution of Enumeration Forms. Despite challenging weather conditions, difficult terrain, scattered habitations, and other logistical constraints, the BLOs made every effort to ensure that no household was left uncovered. The collection, verification, and digitisation of the forms were successfully completed on 17 June 2026.

The District Election Officer Paul L. Khuma, expressed gratitude to all stakeholders for their contributions towards the successful conduct of the ongoing Special Intensive Revision. He acknowledged the dedicated efforts of the Election Branch staff, Electoral Registration Officers (EROs), Assistant Electoral Registration Officers (AEROs), Booth Level Officers (BLOs), Booth Level Agents (BLAs), media, community leaders, and all stakeholders for their valuable support and cooperation.

Attachment

title: Champhai District completes digitization of Enumeration Forms
content:
181/2026-2027

Dated Champhai, 17th June 2026:Amidst the ongoing Special Intensive Revision, all assembly constituencies under Champhai District i.e 23-Champhai North, 24-Champhai South and 25-East Tuipui completed digitization of Enumeration Forms tonight at 8pm.

There are 58,846 voters within Champhai District, with 22,443 voters coming from 24-Champhai South as the constituency with the most voters, followed by 20,435 voters from 23-Champhai North and 15,968 voters from 25-East Tuipui. 21,412 Enumeration Forms were collected from 24-Champhai South, which is 95.41% of Enumeration Forms distributed, with the remaining 1,041 (4.59%) marked as uncollectible as they were ASDD (absent, shifting, death or duplicate). 19,572 Enumeration Forms (95.78%) were collected from 23-Champhai North, and the remaining 853 (4.22%) were marked uncollectible. 15,466 Enumeration Forms (96.86%) were collected from 25-East Tuipui, and the remaining 502 (3.14%) were marked uncollectible. In total, 56,450 Enumeration Forms (95.93%) were collected within Champhai District, and 2,396 (4.07%) were marked uncollectible.

The District Election Officer expressed his gratitude at the timely and efficient completion of digitization of Enumeration Forms, and extended the same to the Dy. DEO, EO, EROs, AEROs, BLO Supervisors, BLOs, Addl. BLOs, NGOs, political parties, the press and influencers for their hard work and dedication. The DEO also asked for the people's cooperation for the remaining phases of SIR.

The Enumeration Phase started on May 30th and is expected to end on June 28th, and rationalization will also take place for polling stations with more than 1,200 voters. The Draft Electoral Roll will be released on July 4th, and the time for hearing of complaints and appeals will be from July 4th - August 4th. These complaints and appeals will be reviewed and dealt with till September 2nd, and the Final Electoral Roll will be released on September 6th.
